session.user:
Add `curated_in: erk-tui` to the YAML frontmatter of the following files. The field should be added as the LAST field before the closing `---` in each file's frontmatter block.
For each file: 1. Read the file to see its current frontmatter 2. Find the closing `---` of the frontmatter block 3. Add `curated_in: erk-tui` as a new line just before the closing `---`
Files to edit:
In /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/: 1. title-truncation-edge-cases.md 2. tui-command-registration.md 3. view-aware-commands.md 4. view-mode-help.md 5. view-switching.md
In /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/textual/: 6. quirks.md 7. background-workers.md 8. testing.md 9. datatable-markup-escaping.md 10. widget-development.md
For example, if a file has: ``` --- title: Some Title read_when: - "some condition" last_audited: "2026-02-05 00:00 PT" audit_result: edited --- ```
It should become: ``` --- title: Some Title read_when: - "some condition" last_audited: "2026-02-05 00:00 PT" audit_result: edited curated_in: erk-tui --- ```
IMPORTANT: The `curated_in: erk-tui` line goes after ALL existing fields, immediately before the closing `---`.
Do NOT modify any content after the frontmatter block.
session.assistant: I'll add `curated_in: erk-tui` to the frontmatter of all the specified files. Let me start by reading each file to see its current frontmatter.

session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: Title Truncation Edge Cases 3→last_audited: "2026-02-16 04:53 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "implementing title truncation in TUI" 7→ - "troubleshooting truncated titles showing only prefix" 8→ - "working with title display lengths" 9→--- 10→ 11→# Title Truncation Edge Cases 12→ 13→Title truncation interacts unexpectedly with prefixes and markup. This document covers edge cases to watch for. 14→ 15→## The Basic Problem 16→ 17→When truncation is applied after prefixing: 18→ 19→``` 20→Original: "Short Title" 21→+ Prefix: "[erk-learn] Short Title" (24 chars) 22→Truncated: "[erk-learn] Short Ti..." (20 chars visible) 23→``` 24→ 25→The `[erk-learn] ` prefix consumes 12 characters of the display budget. 26→ 27→## Edge Cases 28→ 29→### Prefix Longer Than Truncation Limit 30→
... [65 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: TUI Command Registration 3→read_when: 4→ - "adding a new TUI command to the registry" 5→ - "understanding the 3-place coordination pattern for TUI commands" 6→ - "working with TUI command categories or display formatters" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "adding a new TUI command without updating all 3 places" 9→ warning: "TUI commands require 3-place coordination: registry definition, display formatter, and action inventory. See tui-command-registration.md." 10→ - action: "adding a CLI flag that affects behavior without checking TUI command palette" 11→ warning: "TUI command palette generates shell commands via src/erk/tui/commands/registry.py. When adding CLI flags that change behavior, check if TUI-generated commands need the flag too." 12→--- 13→ 14→# TUI Command Registration 15→ 16→Adding a TUI command requires coordinating three places in the codebase. 17→ 18→## 3-Place Coordination Pattern 19→ 20→When adding a command, update all three: 21→ 22→1. **Display formatter**: A `_display_*()` function that generates the display name 23→2. **CommandDefinition**: Entry in `get_all_commands()` with id, name, category, availability, and display getter 24→3. **Action inventory**: Command appears in the correct category 25→ 26→All definitions are in `src/erk/tui/commands/registry.py`. 27→ 28→## Single Source of Truth: `launch_key` Field 29→ 30→<!-- Source: src/erk/tui/commands/types.py:60 -->
... [75 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: View-Aware Command Filtering 3→read_when: 4→ - "registering a new TUI command with view-mode filtering" 5→ - "understanding how commands are filtered by view mode (plans, learn, objectives)" 6→ - "adding objective-specific commands to the command palette" 7→ - "implementing streaming commands in the TUI" 8→tripwires: 9→ - action: "registering a new TUI command without a view-mode predicate" 10→ warning: "Every command must use _is_plan_view() or _is_objectives_view() to prevent it from appearing in the wrong view. Commands without view predicates appear in all views." 11→ - action: "adding streaming commands without using the streaming operation pattern" 12→ warning: "Streaming ACTION commands need the streaming operation wrapper with status bar tracking. See streaming-output.md for the current pattern." 13→--- 14→ 15→# View-Aware Command Filtering 16→ 17→The TUI command palette filters commands by the active view mode. Plan commands appear only in Plans/Learn views; objective commands appear only in the Objectives view. This filtering is implemented through view-mode predicates in the command registry. 18→ 19→## View Mode Predicates 20→ 21→<!-- Source: src/erk/tui/commands/registry.py:21-28 --> 22→ 23→Two predicates partition the command space by view mode: 24→ 25→<!-- See _is_plan_view and _is_objectives_view in src/erk/tui/commands/registry.py:21-28 --> 26→ 27→- `_is_plan_view(ctx)`: Returns `True` when not in Objectives view (i.e., Plans or Learn) — checks `ctx.view_mode != ViewMode.OBJECTIVES` 28→- `_is_objectives_view(ctx)`: Returns `True` when in Objectives view — checks `ctx.view_mode == ViewMode.OBJECTIVES` 29→ 30→The `CommandContext.view_mode` field carries the active `ViewMode` enum value. `MainListCommandProvider` reads this dynamically from `self._app._view_mode`, while `PlanCommandProvider` hardcodes `ViewMode.PLANS` since the detail modal is always in plan context.
... [87 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: View-Mode-Aware Help Screen 3→read_when: 4→ - "modifying the TUI help screen" 5→ - "adding view-specific actions to the TUI" 6→ - "understanding ViewMode-conditional rendering" 7→--- 8→ 9→# View-Mode-Aware Help Screen 10→ 11→The TUI `HelpScreen` accepts a `view_mode: ViewMode` parameter and conditionally renders help content based on the active view (plans vs objectives). 12→ 13→## Implementation Pattern 14→ 15→<!-- Source: src/erk/tui/screens/help_screen.py --> 16→ 17→The `HelpScreen.__init__()` stores `view_mode` as an instance variable. A helper method `_is_objectives_view()` wraps the check. The `compose()` method uses this to branch rendering: 18→ 19→```python 20→def compose(self) -> ComposeResult: 21→ is_objectives = self._is_objectives_view() 22→ # ... shared sections ... 23→ if is_objectives: 24→ yield Label("Enter View objective details", ...) 25→ else: 26→ yield Label("Enter View plan details", ...) 27→``` 28→ 29→## Conditional Sections 30→
... [26 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: TUI View Switching 3→read_when: 4→ - "adding a new view mode to the TUI" 5→ - "understanding how view switching and caching work" 6→ - "debugging data not appearing in a specific view" 7→ - "working with PlanBodyScreen content type parameterization" 8→tripwires: 9→ - action: "adding a new ViewMode without updating VIEW_CONFIGS" 10→ warning: "Every ViewMode must have a corresponding ViewConfig in VIEW_CONFIGS. Missing configs cause KeyError at runtime." 11→ - action: "using _render() as a method name in Textual widgets" 12→ warning: "Textual's LSP reserves _render(). Use _refresh_display() instead (see ViewBar)." 13→ - action: "pushing PlanBodyScreen without explicit content_type" 14→ warning: "Content type must come from view_mode at push time, not derived inside the screen." 15→ - action: "adding labels to ViewConfig.labels expecting OR semantics from GitHub API" 16→ warning: "GitHub GraphQL uses AND semantics for label arrays. Multiple labels in a single ViewConfig.labels means items must have ALL listed labels. Use separate views for separate label types. See github-graphql-label-semantics.md." 17→ - action: "adding the same label to multiple ViewConfig entries without exclude_labels" 18→ warning: "Without exclude_labels, items matching the shared label appear in multiple views. Use exclude_labels for defense-in-depth deduplication (Plans view excludes erk-learn)." 19→last_audited: "2026-02-17 00:00 PT" 20→audit_result: edited 21→--- 22→ 23→# TUI View Switching 24→ 25→The TUI supports three views — Plans, Learn, and Objectives — with instant switching via keyboard shortcuts. This document explains the view system architecture, the two-tier filtering strategy, and the caching mechanism that enables sub-millisecond view switches. 26→ 27→## Core Types 28→ 29→<!-- Source: src/erk/tui/views/types.py --> 30→
... [120 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: Textual API Quirks and Workarounds 3→read_when: 4→ - "working with Textual TUI framework" 5→ - "debugging DataTable, App, or CSS issues in Textual" 6→ - "writing tests for Textual applications" 7→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:30 PT" 8→audit_result: edited 9→--- 10→ 11→# Textual API Quirks and Workarounds 12→ 13→This document captures API quirks discovered while building the erk dash TUI with Textual. 14→ 15→## DataTable Quirks 16→ 17→### cursor_type Must Be Set via `__init__` 18→ 19→**Problem**: `cursor_type` is a `Reactive[CursorType]`, not a plain attribute. Setting it as a class attribute causes type errors: 20→ 21→```python 22→# WRONG - causes type error 23→class MyTable(DataTable): 24→ cursor_type = "row" # Type error: Literal['row'] not assignable to Reactive[CursorType] 25→``` 26→ 27→**Solution**: Pass to `super().__init__()`: 28→ 29→```python 30→# CORRECT
... [212 lines omitted] ...
80→**Problem**: The base `App.action_quit` is an async method. Overriding it with a sync method causes pyright errors about incompatible return types. 86→def action_quit(self) -> None: # Type error: incompatible override 104→**Problem**: When loading data in a background thread with `run_in_executor`, you might try to use `call_from_thread` to update the UI. This fails in test context with "must run in different thread" error. 159→# WRONG - causes MarkupError at render time 163→**Error signature:** 166→MarkupError: Expected markup value (found '://github.com/...') 196→**Problem**: When displaying raw JSON, code, or file contents in a Static widget, `escape_markup()` may not fully prevent `MarkupError`. Content with Python type annotations like `list[str]` or complex nested escape sequences can still cause parsing failures. 198→**Error signature:** 201→MarkupError: Expected markup value (found '\\"number\\"],\\n ...') 221→**Problem**: The StatusBar widget displays subprocess error messages that may contain brackets (e.g., `[error details]`). With default `markup=True`, these brackets are parsed as Rich markup tags, causing `MarkupError` at render time.

1→--- 2→title: Textual Background Workers 3→read_when: 4→ - "running subprocess in TUI without blocking" 5→ - "streaming output to Textual widgets" 6→ - "using run_worker for async operations" 7→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:20 PT" 8→audit_result: clean 9→--- 10→ 11→# Textual Background Workers 12→ 13→## When to Use `run_worker()` 14→ 15→Use `run_worker()` for I/O-bound operations that should not block the UI: 16→ 17→- Subprocess execution with output streaming 18→- Network requests 19→- File operations on large files 20→ 21→## Subprocess Streaming Pattern 22→ 23→```python 24→from textual.worker import Worker, WorkerState 25→import asyncio 26→ 27→def action_execute(self) -> None: 28→ """Start command execution.""" 29→ self._output_panel.visible = True 30→ self.run_worker(self._stream_subprocess, exclusive=True)
... [39 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: Textual TUI Testing Patterns 3→read_when: 4→ - "testing Textual TUI components" 5→ - "writing tests for ModalScreen" 6→ - "testing keyboard bindings" 7→ - "using Pilot API for async tests" 8→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:20 PT" 9→audit_result: clean 10→--- 11→ 12→# Textual TUI Testing Patterns 13→ 14→## Overview 15→ 16→This project uses Textual for TUI applications. Testing Textual apps requires async tests with the Pilot API. 17→ 18→## Basic Test Structure 19→ 20→```python 21→import pytest 22→from erk.tui.app import ErkDashApp, PlanDetailScreen 23→ 24→@pytest.mark.asyncio 25→async def test_tui_behavior() -> None: 26→ """Test TUI interaction.""" 27→ provider = FakePlanDataProvider([make_plan_row(123, "Test")]) 28→ filters = PlanFilters.default() 29→ app = ErkDashApp(provider, filters, refresh_interval=0) 30→
... [90 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: DataTable Rich Markup Escaping 3→last_audited: "2026-02-17 18:30 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "adding cell values to Textual DataTable" 7→ - "displaying user data in DataTable cells" 8→ - "troubleshooting invisible text in DataTable" 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "adding cell values to Textual DataTable" 11→ warning: "Always wrap in `Text(value)` if strings contain user data with brackets. Otherwise `[anything]` will be interpreted as Rich markup." 12→--- 13→ 14→# DataTable Rich Markup Escaping 15→ 16→Textual's DataTable interprets plain strings as Rich markup by default. This causes user data containing brackets (like `[erk-learn]` prefixes) to disappear. 17→ 18→## The Problem 19→ 20→When you pass a plain string to `DataTable.add_row()`, Rich interprets bracket sequences as markup tags: 21→ 22→```python 23→# WRONG: The [prefix] disappears because Rich interprets it as markup 24→table.add_row("[erk-learn] My Plan Title") 25→# Result: "My Plan Title" (prefix invisible) 26→``` 27→ 28→The `[erk-learn]` text is treated as a Rich style tag. Since no style named `erk-learn` exists, Rich silently renders nothing for that portion. 29→ 30→## The Solution
... [60 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: Textual Widget Development Guide 3→read_when: 4→ - "creating Textual widgets" 5→ - "adding ModalScreen dialogs" 6→ - "implementing keyboard bindings" 7→ - "writing Textual CSS styles" 8→last_audited: "2026-02-05 00:00 PT" 9→audit_result: edited 10→--- 11→ 12→# Textual Widget Development 13→ 14→For standard Textual API patterns (ModalScreen, Bindings, compose(), CSS, widget types), consult the [Textual documentation](https://textual.textualize.io/). This doc focuses on erk-specific patterns. 15→ 16→## Erk TUI Source Layout 17→ 18→| Component | Location | 19→| --------- | ----------------------------------- | 20→| Main app | `src/erk/tui/app.py` (`ErkDashApp`) | 21→| Widgets | `src/erk/tui/widgets/` | 22→| Screens | `src/erk/tui/screens/` | 23→| CSS | `src/erk/tui/styles/` | 24→ 25→## Reference Implementations 26→ 27→- **ModalScreens**: `HelpScreen` (`screens/help_screen.py`), `PlanDetailScreen` (`screens/plan_detail_screen.py`), `IssueBodyScreen` (`screens/issue_body_screen.py`) 28→- **Streaming output**: `CommandOutputPanel` (`widgets/command_output.py`) — uses `RichLog` for live output display 29→- **Clickable links**: `widgets/clickable_link.py` — demonstrates accessing app properties from widgets 30→
... [11 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: Now I'll edit each file to add `curated_in: erk-tui` as the last field before the closing `---` in the frontmatter.

session.assistant:
Perfect! I've successfully added `curated_in: erk-tui` to the YAML frontmatter of all 10 files. The field has been added as the last field before the closing `---` in each file's frontmatter block.
## Summary
Successfully edited the following files:
### TUI directory (/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/): 1. **title-truncation-edge-cases.md** - Added `curated_in: erk-tui` after `read_when` 2. **tui-command-registration.md** - Added `curated_in: erk-tui` after `tripwires` 3. **view-aware-commands.md** - Added `curated_in: erk-tui` after `tripwires` 4. **view-mode-help.md** - Added `curated_in: erk-tui` after `read_when` 5. **view-switching.md** - Added `curated_in: erk-tui` after `audit_result`
### Textual directory (/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/textual/): 6. **quirks.md** - Added `curated_in: erk-tui` after `audit_result` 7. **background-workers.md** - Added `curated_in: erk-tui` after `audit_result` 8. **testing.md** - Added `curated_in: erk-tui` after `audit_result` 9. **datatable-markup-escaping.md** - Added `curated_in: erk-tui` after `tripwires` 10. **widget-development.md** - Added `curated_in: erk-tui` after `audit_result`
All files now have the `curated_in: erk-tui` field positioned correctly as the last field before the closing `---` in their YAML frontmatter, without modifying any content after the frontmatter block.
